item.title: How Cheech Marin helped the ‘Godfather of West Coast graffiti’ break into the art world
item.description: A new art exhibition reunites the actor and comedian (and advocate of Chicano art) with Los Angeles graffiti artist Chaz Bojórquez and declares: 'Chicano art is American art.'

item.title: Why aren't hotels full for the World Cup — and what does it mean for LA?

item.title: Homicides are on decline in LA but shooting deaths of unhoused people remain disproportionately high

item.title: Israeli leader who pulled out of Lebanon warns against getting stuck again

Jay Obernolte (R-CA-23) introduced the Disaster Ready Infrastructure Act, legislation which requires state Departments of Transportation and Metropolitan Planning Organizations to identify roads, bridges, and other transportation assets that are particularly vulnerable to natural disasters, as well as those that are essential to emergency operations […]

item.title: Meet the team that brings color to the Metro system

item.description:

item.title: Vandenberg executes back-to-back space, ICBM launches as operations deputy marks final mission
item.description: Executing a dual-launch night demands relentless precision. While strategic nuclear deterrence missions like the unarmed Minuteman III test are scheduled years in advance, spaceport launches are dictated by the requirements of orbital mechanics. When those timelines intersect, the rapid pivot from deploying orbital assets to testing the nation’s nuclear deterrent leaves no room for error.
item.source:markdown:
Executing a dual-launch night demands relentless precision. While strategic nuclear deterrence missions like the unarmed Minuteman III test are scheduled years in advance, spaceport launches are dictated by the requirements of orbital mechanics. When those timelines intersect, the rapid pivot from deploying orbital assets to testing the nation’s nuclear deterrent leaves no room for error.
![U.S. Space Force Col. Dorian Hatcher looks out over the launch operations floor.](https://media.defense.gov/2026/May/21/2003935858/300/300/0/260520-X-EI087-1002.JPG)

item.title: Homelessness Holds Steady Across Three L.A. Neighborhoods as Rough Sleeping Reaches a Four-Year High
item.description: The overall number of unsheltered people in Hollywood, Skid Row, and Venice remained flat in 2025. But rough sleeping—sleeping without a tent, vehicle, or other shelter—rose by 20 percent.
